title: oxalis stricta f. cymosa
intro: Oxalis stricta f. cymosa, also known as the woodland sorrel or lesser wood sorrel, is a plant species in the wood-sorrel family that is native to North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, making it a potential treatment for various health issues. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive problems, and supporting the immune system. Oxalis stricta f. cymosa can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or salves, and its leaves and roots are often used in traditional medicine.
